# AWS Best Practices Skill

AWS Best Practices Skill is an installable Claude Code and OpenAI Codex skill
that provides source-linked AWS best practices for 208 AWS services and 9
cross-service topics.

This is an independent community-maintained project. It is not an official AWS
skill, AWS product, or AWS-maintained resource, and it is not affiliated with
or endorsed by Amazon Web Services.

## Primary Files

- Repository: https://github.com/ferdinandobons/AWSBestPracticesSkill
- Skill router: https://github.com/ferdinandobons/AWSBestPracticesSkill/blob/main/SKILL.md
- Human catalog: https://github.com/ferdinandobons/AWSBestPracticesSkill/blob/main/catalog.md
- Machine catalog: https://github.com/ferdinandobons/AWSBestPracticesSkill/blob/main/catalog.json
- Generation process: https://github.com/ferdinandobons/AWSBestPracticesSkill/blob/main/GENERATE.md
- Update process: https://github.com/ferdinandobons/AWSBestPracticesSkill/blob/main/REFRESH.md
- Maintenance guide: https://github.com/ferdinandobons/AWSBestPracticesSkill/blob/main/MAINTENANCE.md

## Scope

Use this repository when answering questions about recommended ways to secure,
run, optimize, operate, or review AWS services. The corpus intentionally
contains only best practices, with official AWS source links. It does not
contain pricing tables, service introductions, tutorials, or extended code
walkthroughs.

Answer behavior: use the local catalog first. For a specific scenario or
workload, select only the practices that apply to that case. If no specific
scenario is provided, give a general production baseline for the service. Do
not browse or re-check AWS documentation for ordinary answers unless the user
explicitly requests live verification or the local catalog is missing the
service/topic.
